description_long: This is an irregularly shaped, light beige rock with a rough, porous surface and a somewhat lumpy, granular texture. The rock appears to be composed of a soft sedimentary material, possibly limestone or a similar calcareous stone, with small inclusions and uneven protrusions throughout. Its surface is matte and unpolished, with visible pits and crevices that give it a weathered, natural appearance. The coloration is consistent across all sides, with subtle variations in tone due to shadows and surface undulations. The rock is compact and solid, with no visible cracks or breaks, and its size suggests it could be easily moved by a person. This type of rock would commonly be found in urban parks, gardens, or as part of landscaping features along sidewalks or pathways.
description: A light beige, rough-textured rock with a lumpy, porous surface and natural, weathered appearance. Its irregular shape and matte finish suggest it is a sedimentary stone, likely used in landscaping or as an obstacle.
description_view_0: This view shows a prominent, jagged protrusion on the upper left and a generally uneven, bumpy surface.
description_view_1: This side appears flatter and more compact, with fewer deep crevices and a more uniform texture.
description_view_2: The rock is seen at an angle, highlighting its elongated form and the rough, granular surface along the edge.
description_view_3: This view presents the broadest face, with a rounded, lumpy surface and several small inclusions visible.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped sidewalk edge
